# Agile Ceremonies Checklist

**Owner:** [Agile Coach](../octoacme-roles-and-personas.md#agile-coach)  
**Used by:** Agile Coach, Project Manager, Developers, Product Manager, Tech Lead  
**When to use:** Reference this checklist when preparing for or facilitating agile ceremonies. Use the preparation items before each ceremony and the health-check section at the end of each sprint to assess team process quality.

---

## Sprint Planning

**Facilitated by:** Agile Coach / Project Manager  
**Attendees:** Development team, Product Manager, Tech Lead

### Preparation
- [ ] Product backlog is groomed and prioritized by Product Manager
- [ ] Team capacity for the sprint is confirmed
- [ ] Previous sprint velocity reviewed to inform capacity
- [ ] Definition of Done reviewed and agreed

### During the Ceremony
- [ ] Sprint goal defined and agreed by the team
- [ ] Backlog items selected to fill sprint capacity
- [ ] Each item has clear acceptance criteria (validated with [Business Analyst](../octoacme-roles-and-personas.md#business-analyst) if needed)
- [ ] Tasks broken down and assigned (or self-selected)
- [ ] Risks or blockers identified and logged

### Outputs
- [ ] Sprint backlog committed
- [ ] Sprint goal documented
- [ ] Team board updated

---

## Daily Standup

**Facilitated by:** Agile Coach (or team self-facilitates)  
**Attendees:** Development team, Tech Lead, Project Manager

### Guidelines
- [ ] Meeting is timeboxed (15 minutes or less)
- [ ] Each team member answers: What did I do yesterday? What will I do today? Are there any blockers?
- [ ] Blockers are noted and assigned an owner for follow-up
- [ ] Parking-lot items moved out of standup for separate discussion

---

## Sprint Review / Demo

**Facilitated by:** Agile Coach / Project Manager  
**Attendees:** Development team, Product Manager, Stakeholder Sponsor (optional), QA/Test Lead

### Preparation
- [ ] Completed items are demo-ready in a stable environment
- [ ] QA/Test Lead has signed off on demoed items
- [ ] Agenda circulated to stakeholders in advance

### During the Ceremony
- [ ] Sprint goal reviewed — achieved or not?
- [ ] Each completed item demonstrated against acceptance criteria
- [ ] Stakeholder feedback captured
- [ ] Incomplete items documented and moved to backlog

### Outputs
- [ ] Demo feedback recorded
- [ ] Incomplete items returned to backlog with notes

---

## Sprint Retrospective

**Facilitated by:** Agile Coach  
**Attendees:** Development team, Tech Lead, Project Manager

### Preparation
- [ ] Action items from previous retrospective reviewed
- [ ] Retrospective format chosen (e.g., Start/Stop/Continue, 4Ls, Mad/Sad/Glad)
- [ ] Safe and blameless environment established

### During the Ceremony
- [ ] What went well? (celebrate successes)
- [ ] What could be improved? (identify friction points)
- [ ] Root causes explored for recurring issues
- [ ] Actionable improvement items identified (limit to 2–3 per sprint)
- [ ] Each action item has an owner and due date

### Outputs
- [ ] Retrospective notes published to team
- [ ] Action items logged in the backlog or task tracker
- [ ] Working agreements updated if needed

---

## Backlog Refinement (Grooming)

**Facilitated by:** Agile Coach / Product Manager  
**Attendees:** Product Manager, Business Analyst, Tech Lead, Development team (representative)

### Preparation
- [ ] Product Manager has drafted or updated top backlog items
- [ ] Business Analyst has clarified requirements and acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tech Lead has reviewed items for technical feasibility

### During the Ceremony
- [ ] Items estimated (story points or t-shirt sizes)
- [ ] Acceptance criteria reviewed and agreed
- [ ] Dependencies and risks identified
- [ ] Items too large are split into smaller stories
- [ ] Backlog priority confirmed for upcoming sprints

### Outputs
- [ ] Backlog items estimated and ready for sprint planning
- [ ] Priority order confirmed

---

## Agile Health Check (End of Sprint)

Use this quick assessment to identify process improvement areas.

| Area | Rating (1–5) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Sprint goal clarity | | |
| Backlog quality (well-defined items) | | |
| Ceremony effectiveness (value vs. time) | | |
| Team collaboration and communication | | |
| Blockers resolved quickly | | |
| Definition of Done consistently applied | | |
| Retrospective actions completed | | |

**Overall sprint health:** `🟢 Healthy` / `🟡 Needs attention` / `🔴 Struggling`  
**Top improvement action for next sprint:**
